content.content:
"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

content.text: This is a single-file change to `outlines/types/__init__.py`. Let me start the coordination protocol.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "SURVEY" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Adding hash_sha256 = Regex pattern for SHA-256 hash validation, appended near other Regex type definitions (isbn, email, etc.)"

content.content: sent to agent1

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "CLAIM" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "I will own outlines/types/__init__.py and implement both hash_sha256 and your ssn type. Please DEFER and send SPEC for ssn."

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Both of us claimed the same file simultaneously. To resolve, I'll concede ownership to agent1 and send a DEFER with a precise SPEC for `hash_sha256`.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "DEFER" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --spec "Add a new module-level variable named hash_sha256 in outlines/types/__init__.py, placed after the existing isbn Regex definition (near the end of the file). Definition: hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'). This must be an instance of the Regex class from outlines.types.dsl (already imported at top of file). No other imports are needed. Behavior required: hash_sha256.matches('e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855') == True (accepts exactly 64 hex chars, any case mix); rejects strings that are shorter (63 chars) or longer (65 chars) than 64, strings containing non-hex characters (g-z, special chars, spaces), empty strings, and strings with leading/trailing whitespace. Since Regex.matches uses fullmatch semantics via the DSL, the raw pattern [a-fA-F0-9]{64} is sufficient (no need for ^/$ anchors) as long as Term.validate/matches uses fullmatch -- please verify against how isbn/email types validate in this same file so behavior is consistent with other Regex types. hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__ should yield {'type': 'string', 'pattern': '[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'} inherited automatically from Regex base class, no extra code needed." --note "hash_sha256 feature - single line addition, no other files touched"

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Our concession messages crossed. Agent1's latest message explicitly defers to me with a SPEC for `ssn` — I'll accept that as final and confirm ownership.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "CLAIM" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Acknowledged - I own outlines/types/__init__.py. Will implement both ssn (per your SPEC: ssn = Regex(r'\\d{3}-\\d{2}-\\d{4}') after isbn) and hash_sha256. Ignore my earlier DEFER message, it crossed with yours - final resolution: I am owner."

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: We have duplicate claims crossing. I'll settle this definitively — agent1's last message firmly claims ownership and echoes my spec correctly, so I'll stand down as owner.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "DEFER" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --spec "hash_sha256 = Regex(r'[a-fA-F0-9]{64}'), placed after isbn definition, no other imports needed. Should validate fullmatch of exactly 64 hex chars any case." --note "FINAL: conceding ownership to agent1 to break the race - you own outlines/types/__init__.py, I will not touch it. Please implement both ssn and hash_sha256 there and send DONE when finished."

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Good, this matches my last message. I'll confirm receipt and wait for DONE.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "CLAIM" --files "outlines/types/__init__.py" --note "Confirmed and acknowledged - agent1 owns outlines/types/__init__.py per tie-break rule (agent1 < agent2). I will not edit this file. Waiting for your DONE."

content.content: sent to agent1

content.text: Agent1 has completed the implementation. Let me verify the file now contains `hash_sha256` correctly.

content.content: (Bash completed with no output)

"""Output types for structured generation and regex DSL."""

from dataclasses import dataclass, is_dataclass
from enum import Enum, EnumMeta
from typing import Union

from jsonschema import Draft202012Validator as Validator
from jsonschema.exceptions import SchemaError
from pydantic import BaseModel, TypeAdapter
from typing_extensions import _TypedDictMeta  # type: ignore

from . import airports, countries, locale
from outlines.types.dsl import (
    Regex,
    CFG,
    FSM,
    JsonSchema,
    regex,
    cfg,
    fsm,
    json_schema,
    optional,
    either,
    exactly,
    at_least,
    at_most,
    between,
    zero_or_more,
    one_or_more,
    # deprecated
    repeat,
    times,
)


# Python types
string = Regex(r'"[^"]*"')
integer = Regex(r"[+-]?(0|[1-9][0-9]*)")
boolean = Regex("(True|False)")
number = Regex(rf"{integer.pattern}(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-][0-9]+)?")
date = Regex(r"(\d{4})-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])-([0-2][0-9]|3[0-1])")
time = Regex(r"([0-1][0-9]|2[0-3]):([0-5][0-9]):([0-5][0-9])")
datetime = Regex(rf"({date.pattern})(\s)({time.pattern})")

# Basic regex types
digit = Regex(r"\d")
char = Regex(r"\w")
newline = Regex(r"(\r\n|\r|\n)")  # Matched new lines on Linux, Windows & MacOS
whitespace = Regex(r"\s")

# Document-specific types
sentence = Regex(r"[A-Z].*\s*[.!?]")
paragraph = Regex(rf"{sentence.pattern}(?:\s+{sentence.pattern})*\n+")


# The following regex is FRC 5322 compliant and was found at:
# https://emailregex.com/
email = Regex(
    r"""(?:[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+(?:\.[a-z0-9!#$%&'*+/=?^_`{|}~-]+)*|"(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21\x23-\x5b\x5d-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])*")@(?:(?:[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?\.)+[a-z0-9](?:[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?|\[(?:(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9]))\.){3}(?:(2(5[0-5]|[0-4][0-9])|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])|[a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]:(?:[\x01-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\x21-\x5a\x53-\x7f]|\\[\x01-\x09\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x7f])+)\])"""
)

# Matches any ISBN number. Note that this is not completely correct as not all
# 10 or 13 digits numbers are valid ISBNs. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISBN
# Taken from O'Reilly's Regular Expression Cookbook:
# https://www.oreilly.com/library/view/regular-expressions-cookbook/9781449327453/ch04s13.html
#
# TODO: The check digit can only be computed by calling a function to compute it dynamically
isbn = Regex(
    r"(?:ISBN(?:-1[03])?:? )?(?=[0-9X]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){3})[- 0-9X]{13}$|97[89][0-9]{10}$|(?=(?:[0-9]+[- ]){4})[- 0-9]{17}$)(?:97[89][- ]?)?[0-9]{1,5}[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9]+[- ]?[0-9X]"
)

lines: +# Matches a SHA-256 hash: exactly 64 hexadecimal characters

content.text: Now let me verify it works correctly.

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256

valid = 'e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855'
print(len(valid))
print(hash_sha256.matches(valid[:64]))
print(hash_sha256.matches('a'*63))
print(hash_sha256.matches('a'*65))
print(hash_sha256.matches('g'*64))
print(hash_sha256.matches(''))
print(hash_sha256.matches(' '+'a'*64))
print(hash_sha256.__get_pydantic_json_schema__(None, None))
"

input.command:
python -c "
from outlines.types import hash_sha256
from pydantic import BaseModel

class FileRecord(BaseModel):
    filename: str
    checksum: hash_sha256

r = FileRecord(filename='a.txt', checksum='e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b85')
print(r)
try:
    FileRecord(filename='a.txt', checksum='not-a-hash')
except Exception as e:
    print('correctly rejected:', type(e).__name__)
"

from outlines.types import hash_sha256
